title: Nested efficient congruencing and relatives of Vinogradov's mean value theorem

thm:1.1.summary: The full main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem, by nested efficient congruencing: for $k\ge2$ and $s\ge1$, $J_{s,k}(X)\ll X^{\epsilon}(X^s+X^{2s-\frac12k(k+1)})$, with the sharp asymptotic $J_{s,k}(X)\asymp X^{2s-\frac12k(k+1)}$ for $s\ge\tfrac12k(k+1)$. A second, purely arithmetic, proof of the theorem proved by Bourgain–Demeter–Guth via decoupling — and the engine of the applications below.

cor:1.2.summary: Asymptotic form: for $s>\tfrac12k(k+1)$, $J_{s,k}(X)\sim\mathfrak{S}_{s,k}\mathfrak{J}_{s,k}X^{2s-\frac12k(k+1)}$ with the expected singular series and integral — Hardy–Littlewood asymptotics for the Vinogradov system in the optimal range.

thm:3.1.summary: The central technical theorem: a bound for the conditioned mean values driving the congruencing iteration, established by a (nested) induction whose conclusion, fed back, forces the critical exponent. thm:1.1 is deduced from it by standard reductions.

cor:3.2.summary: The mean-value bound at the critical number of variables extracted from thm:3.1 — the form directly implying the main conjecture thm:1.1.

lem:4.1.summary: Properties of translation-dilation invariant families of exponential sums: the algebraic structure (closure under affine changes of variable in the underlying tuple) that makes the conditioning meaningful.

lem:4.2.summary: Further structural lemma on translation-dilation invariant families — the bookkeeping identities used when passing between congruence levels.

lem:5.1.summary: Base of the induction, the trivial case $k=1$: the linear Vinogradov system, where the mean value is computed exactly. Anchors the induction on degree.

lem:6.1.summary: Initial conditioning: replace the raw exponential sum by a 'well-conditioned' one supported on residues with controlled spacing modulo a prime power, at negligible cost. The first move of efficient congruencing.

lem:6.2.summary: Quantitative form of the conditioning loss — how much is given up when restricting to the conditioned residue classes, in terms of the prime and the congruence level.

lem:6.3.summary: Auxiliary congruence-counting estimate underlying the conditioning step (a Hensel/Jacobian-type count of solutions in the relevant residue classes).

lem:7.1.summary: Harnessing approximate translation-dilation invariance: even when a family is only approximately invariant under the affine action, the mean value transforms predictably up to a controlled error. The flexibility that makes the *nested* iteration possible (rather than the single-grade iteration of earlier efficient-congruencing papers).

lem:8.1.summary: The iterative step (first part): one cycle of nested efficient congruencing — descend simultaneously through several congruence levels ('multigrade'), producing a combination of deeper-level conditioned mean values with improved exponents.

lem:8.2.summary: The iterative step (second part): the precise bookkeeping inequality combining the multigrade descent with the approximate-invariance lemma — the recursion that, iterated, yields thm:3.1.

lem:9.1.summary: Distillation (first part): convert a multigrade combination of conditioned mean values into a single monograde quantity, so the iteration can be closed by an ordinary descent. The step that makes 'nested' congruencing tractable.

lem:9.2.summary: Distillation (second part): the convexity/Hölder manipulation realizing the multigrade-to-monograde reduction with the right exponents.

lem:9.3.summary: Distillation (third part): closing estimate ensuring the monograde process inherits the gain — the final ingredient of the descent.

thm:11.1.summary: Solutions of congruences in short intervals: optimal bounds on the number of solutions of the Vinogradov congruence system with variables confined to a short interval, deduced from the mean-value theorem.

cor:11.2.summary: A clean special case of thm:11.1 in the form most often applied.

thm:13.1.summary: Tarry's problem: the least $s$ for which the system $\sum x_i^j=\sum y_i^j$ ($1\le j\le k$) has non-trivial solutions is $s=k+1$ for $k\ge2$ (matching the lower bound) — a clean consequence of the sharp mean value.

thm:14.1.summary: Analogue of Hua's lemma and Waring's problem: improved bounds for $\oint|\sum_{n\le X}e(\alpha n^k)|^t\,d\alpha$ and hence for $G(k)$ (the Waring number), via the Vinogradov mean value theorem.

thm:15.1.summary: Vinogradov's mean value theorem in number fields: the analogue of thm:1.1 with rational integers replaced by the integers of a number field, by transliterating the nested efficient-congruencing argument.

thm:16.1.summary: Multidimensional analogues via restriction of scalars: a Vinogradov-type mean value theorem for systems indexed by points of $\mathbb{Z}^d$, obtained from the number-field case by Weil restriction.

thm:17.1.summary: Vinogradov's mean value theorem in function fields $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$: the function-field analogue of thm:1.1, by the function-field version of nested efficient congruencing.

edges.note: The main conjecture follows from the critical-variables mean-value bound by standard interpolation over the number of variables.
The asymptotic formula is the upper bound combined with the (easy) lower bound and a circle-method singular-series analysis.
Specialization of the central technical theorem to the critical number of variables.
Proved by iterating the nested efficient-congruencing step to (effective) infinity.
Closing the iteration requires distilling the multigrade combinations into a single monograde process.
The induction on degree is anchored at the trivial case $k=1$.
The iteration is launched from the initial conditioning of the raw exponential sum.
The multigrade descent step exploits approximate translation-dilation invariance.
The bookkeeping inequality is built on the descent step.
The conditioning-loss estimate enters the cost accounting of each cycle.
Each descent stays within a translation-dilation invariant family.
Distillation operates on the multigrade combinations produced by the iterative step.
The convexity manipulation realizing the multigrade-to-monograde reduction.
Final closing estimate of the distillation.
Approximate invariance is quantified using the structural lemmas on invariant families.
The conditioning relies on the auxiliary congruence-solution count.
Same count, used to bound the conditioning loss.
Conditioning is set up inside a translation-dilation invariant family.
Short-interval congruence counts are read off from the conditioned mean-value bound.
A packaged special case.
The optimal exponent in Tarry's problem follows from the sharp Vinogradov mean value.
Hua-type bounds and the Waring number $G(k)$ improve via the mean value theorem.
The number-field mean value theorem is proved by transliterating nested efficient congruencing to $\mathcal{O}_K$.
The multidimensional analogue is obtained from the number-field case by restriction of scalars.
The function-field mean value theorem is the $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$ version of nested efficient congruencing.

main_results.description: The full main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem and its asymptotic form.

central_technical_theorem.description: The conditioned-mean-value bound that the bulk of the paper proves and from which the main conjecture follows.

invariant_families_and_conditioning.description: Translation-dilation invariant families, the initial conditioning of the exponential sum, and the base case k=1.

nested_iteration_and_distillation.description: Approximate translation-dilation invariance, the multigrade descent step, and the distillation of multigrade combinations into closable monograde processes.

applications.description: Solutions of congruences in short intervals, Tarry's problem, Hua-type estimates and Waring's problem, and Vinogradov's mean value theorem in number fields, multidimensional settings, and function fields.

Wooley:Nested-precursor.title: The cubic case of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem

BDG.title: Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three

Wooley:EfficientCongruencing.title: Vinogradov's mean value theorem via efficient congruencing
